spec: The following excerpt(s) are from the Modelica Language Specification. Use them as authoritative reference when answering the query. Normative text defines the language. Text in brackets [...] is non-normative and only explanatory. An excerpt may represent only part of a section, including partial non-normative text. If your answer quotes or is derived from these excerpts, include this attribution at the end of the answer: 'Derived from the Modelica® Language Specification. Copyright © Modelica Association. Used under CC BY-SA 4.0.'
docs:
The following excerpts come from Wolfram System Modeler documentation, including tutorials, the user guide, and release notes.
Use these excerpts to help users build, modify, simulate, and analyze models in Wolfram System Modeler.
The documentation is copyright Wolfram Research, Inc. When your answer quotes or is derived from these excerpts, cite the source URL(s) on reference.wolfram.com so the user can find the original.
msl: Modelica Standard Library (MSL 4.0) class reference: documentation plus annotation-stripped source code. The code excerpts are authoritative for exact parameter names, defaults, and connect() wiring — prefer them over memory when building models. Entries are keyed by leaf class name; the URL's first path segment is the top-level package (the full dotted path within it is not recorded).
